title: Protein Concentration and Buffer Exchange Using Ultrafiltration for Phase Separation Studies
author: Jianxiong Chen
materials: Basal Buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5, 2 mM DTT, 150 mM NaCl)
Stock Solutions PEG6000 stock: 20% and/or 50% (w/v) NaCl stock: 2 M Base buffer: 10 mM Tris-HCl, 2 mM DTT

steps.title: This protocol describes the concentration and...
steps.instruction: This protocol describes the concentration and buffer exchange of recombinant proteins after Ni-NTA purification using centrifugal ultrafiltration devices. The procedure removes imidazole and excess salts and prepares proteins in a defined low-salt buffer suitable for in vitro phase separation assays under varying NaCl and PEG conditions.

steps.title: Ultrafiltration devices (0
steps.instruction: Ultrafiltration devices (0.5 mL, 10 kDa MWCO, e.g., Amicon‱ Ultra-0.5) Refrigerated centrifuge (capable of 10,000–14,000 × g) Ice bath Pipettes and low-retention tips Liquid nitrogen −80 °C freezer

steps.title: Basal Buffer (Low-salt buffer) 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7
steps.instruction: Basal Buffer (Low-salt buffer) 10 mM Tris-HCl, pH 7.5 2 mM DTT 150 mM NaCl Proteins were maintained in 10 mM Tris-HCl (pH 7.5), 2 mM DTT, and 150 mM NaCl prior to phase separation assays. Used for buffer exchange and as base condition for phase separation assays. The NaCl concentration in the storage buffer was optimized individually for each protein to maintain solubility and stability.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.5 mL ultrapure water to the filter unit.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 2–5 min
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 2–5 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 14,000 × g, 4 °C for 5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 14,000 × g, 4 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: Add up to 0
steps.instruction: Add up to 0.5 mL protein solution (Ni-NTA elution) to the filter unit.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000–14,000 × g, 4 °C for 3–5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000–14,000 × g, 4 °C for 3–5 min.

steps.title: Add 300 μL basal buffer, gently mix
steps.instruction: Add 300 μL basal buffer, gently mix.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 10,000–14,000 × g, 4 °C for 3–5 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 10,000–14,000 × g, 4 °C for 3–5 min.

steps.title: Repeat this wash step three times...
steps.instruction: Repeat this wash step three times to remove imidazole and adjust buffer composition.

steps.title: Final concentration step
steps.instruction: Final concentration step: Centrifuge for 5–15 min until ~50–80 μL remains.

steps.title: Invert the filter device into a clean collection tube
steps.instruction: Invert the filter device into a clean collection tube.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000 × g, 4 °C for 2 min
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000 × g, 4 °C for 2 min.

steps.title: Collect concentrated protein
steps.instruction: Collect concentrated protein.

steps.title: Measure protein concentration immediately
steps.instruction: Measure protein concentration immediately.

steps.title: Store at −80 °C
steps.instruction: Store at −80 °C.

steps.title: Experimental Variables PEG6000
steps.instruction: Experimental Variables PEG6000: 0%, 2%, 5% NaCl: 75–300 mM gradient Protein concentration: defined range depending on experimental design Final reaction volume: 10 μL

steps.title: Mix protein, PEG, NaCl, and buffer to desired final concentrations
steps.instruction: Mix protein, PEG, NaCl, and buffer to desired final concentrations.

steps.title: Incubate briefly at room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate briefly at room temperature.

steps.title: Observe under microscope:
steps.instruction: Observe under microscope:

steps.title: Protein Quality and Buffer Effects Ni-NTA...
steps.instruction: Protein Quality and Buffer Effects Ni-NTA eluates typically contain imidazole , which can interfere with phase separation; thorough buffer exchange is essential. Maintain proteins in a low-salt basal buffer before applying NaCl gradients to ensure accurate control of ionic strength. Avoid using buffers containing glycerol or detergents unless required.

steps.title: Centrifugation and Concentration Use low acceleration/deceleration...
steps.instruction: Centrifugation and Concentration Use low acceleration/deceleration settings to prevent protein aggregation. Do not centrifuge to dryness ; always retain a minimal volume (~50 μL). Monitor for precipitation or turbidity , which may indicate instability.

steps.title: Handling of Ultrafiltration Devices Avoid touching...
steps.instruction: Handling of Ultrafiltration Devices Avoid touching the membrane with pipette tips. Protein adsorption to the membrane may occur, especially at low concentrations. Reuse of filters is not recommended for critical experiments due to contamination risks.

steps.title: DTT Stability DTT should be freshly...
steps.instruction: DTT Stability DTT should be freshly added to buffers before use. Avoid long-term storage of DTT-containing buffers.

steps.title: Phase Separation Assay Design Ensure that...
steps.instruction: Phase Separation Assay Design Ensure that NaCl contribution from protein stock is considered when calculating final concentrations. PEG and NaCl stocks should be prepared in compatible buffers. Work quickly to minimize protein degradation and oxidation.

steps.title: Storage and Freeze–Thaw Avoid repeated freeze–thaw cycles
steps.instruction: Storage and Freeze–Thaw Avoid repeated freeze–thaw cycles. Aliquot into small volumes for single use.
